title: Networks & Internet Systems

prerequisites.text: Admission into the Master's degree specialising in Computer Science, or permission from the course convener. Working knowledge of computer networks.

outline: The objective is to gain advanced understanding of techniques for traffic engineering and quality of service in the Internet architecture. Course content includes: New Network and Transport Protocols (IPv6, Mobile IP, IP Multicast, Multipath TCP, QUIC); Routing and Traffic Engineering; Traffic Engineering with Overlay Networking (MPLS/GMPLS, SDN, NFV); Internet Measurements (QoS and QoE, IP Traffic Monitoring and Analysis). Selected topics may include Cloud Infrastructure, Content Delivery Networks, Internet Access in the Developing World, and Online Data Protection.
assessment: Assignments: 40%; Discussion sessions: 15%; Active Participation in Class: 5%; Final Exam: 40%.
